metadata.crispro_angle: CrisPRO maps the MSS CRC immunotherapy landscape — identifying combination partners, biomarker gaps, and competitive threats relevant to whole-cell vaccine programs

key_mss_talks.speaker: Luis A. Diaz Jr.
key_mss_talks.affiliation: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, Solid Tumor Oncology Division
key_mss_talks.title: Tumor-agnostic PD-1 blockade, ctDNA‑guided MRD and organ‑preserving immunotherapy
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['All 49 MMR‑deficient rectal cancer patients achieved a clinical complete response after 6\u202fmonths of pembrolizumab, enabling non‑operative management (100% cCR).', 'Two‑year recurrence‑free survival was 96/49 (≈96%) in the rectal cohort and 85% in the 54‑patient non‑rectal cohort treated with
open_questions: Is a phase III randomized trial required to establish neoadjuvant PD‑1 blockade as standard of care for organ‑preserving treatment of MMR‑deficient rectal cancer?
What are the mechanistic determinants of the observed tissue‑specific variability in response (e.g., prostate vs. bladder)?
Can earlier or longer duration of PD‑1 blockade further improve cure rates in non‑rectal MMR‑deficient cancers?

key_mss_talks.speaker: Dr. Faye Kastrinos
key_mss_talks.affiliation: Columbia University, Department of Medicine, GI Cancer Genetics and Prevention Program
key_mss_talks.title: Genotype‑specific risk assessment and screening strategies for Lynch syndrome
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['Lynch syndrome prevalence is ~1/350 in the US, with ~63\u202f% of carriers lacking any personal or family cancer history that would trigger clinical suspicion.', 'MSH6 and PMS2 carriers constitute the majority of pathogenic variants in population‑based cohorts, yet they have later onset and lower
open_questions: How will emerging blood‑based multi‑cancer early detection tests perform in Lynch syndrome carriers?
What are the optimal aspirin dosing and duration for each MMR genotype?
Can universal germline testing be operationalized in routine oncology practice to capture the ~1 million undiagnosed carriers?

key_mss_talks.speaker: Dr. Ignacio Melero
key_mss_talks.affiliation: University Clinic of Navarra, Spain; University of Oxford, UK
key_mss_talks.title: FAP‑targeted 4‑1BB ligand trimer amplifies T‑cell engager in MSS colorectal cancer
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['Combination yielded an overall response rate of 13% (20% at highest 4‑1BB ligand dose) in heavily pretreated MSS colorectal cancer.', 'Disease control rate reached 50%, exceeding historical monotherapy with civisatuximab.', 'CRS was mostly grade 1‑2; grade ≥3 CRS occurred in 2% of patients, indica
open_questions: What is the optimal dosing schedule for the FAP‑4‑1BB ligand to maximize efficacy while minimizing toxicity?
Can additional biomarkers (e.g., baseline immune infiltrate) better predict responders?

key_mss_talks.speaker: Yanrong Jiang
key_mss_talks.title: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab stratified by tumor mutation burden and high-risk stage two, three deficient mismatch repair or MSI-high colorectal cancer: The NeoPRISM-CRC study
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['Nine weeks of neoadjuvant pembrolizumab achieved a 59% pCR rate in high-risk stage II/III MMRd CRC (n=31).', 'ctDNA was detected in 100% of patients at baseline, with 24% in the ultrasensitive range (<100 ppm).', "100% of 'super molecular responders' (rapid clearance after 1 cycle) achieved pCR.",
open_questions: Whether super molecular responders can be safely managed non-operatively.

key_mss_talks.speaker: Junyoung Wang
key_mss_talks.title: NAT10‑MYC loop drives autophagy‑dependent MHC‑I downregulation and immunotherapy resistance in MSS colorectal cancer
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['NAT10 is overexpressed in MSS CRC and predicts worse overall survival.', 'NAT10 knockout increases CD8+ T‑cell infiltration and tumor regression in mice.', 'Combination of NAT10 inhibition with anti‑PD‑1 yields complete tumor regression in 80% of mice.', 'MYC transcriptionally up‑regulates NAT10;
open_questions: Will NAT10 inhibition synergize with other immunotherapies (e.g., CTLA‑4 blockade) in MSS CRC?
What biomarkers best predict response to NAT10‑targeted therapy in patients?

key_mss_talks.speaker: Scott Kopetz
key_mss_talks.affiliation: University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center, Department of Gastrointestinal Medical Oncology
key_mss_talks.title: Targeting RAS and RAF in MSS Metastatic Colorectal Cancer
key_mss_talks.key_findings: ['BreakWater first‑line BRAF V600E + EGFR inhibition plus FOLFOX achieved ORR 67% vs 37% with standard chemotherapy (HR for PFS 0.53, HR for OS 0.49).', 'Median OS doubled from 15.1\u202fmonths (control) to 30.3\u202fmonths with EC+FOLFOX in BRAF V600E MSS colorectal cancer.', 'ctDNA clearance of th
open_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of BRAF‑EGFR therapy with cytotoxic chemotherapy to minimize cross‑resistance?
Can pan‑RAS or KRAS G12D inhibitors achieve durable responses without EGFR blockade, or is EGFR inhibition always required?
What are the mechanisms driving the indolent biology of post‑BreakWater resistant tumors (PFS2), and can they be therapeutically exploited?

talks.speaker: Yanrong Jiang
talks.title: neoadjuvant pembrolizumab stratified by tumor mutation burden and high-risk stage two, three deficient mismatch repair or MSI-high colorectal cancer. The NeoPRISM-CRC study

crispro_opportunities.description: Integration of baseline TCR clonal expansion and dynamic ctDNA clearance to predict pCR in MMRd CRC.
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: dynamic ctDNA clearance adds a strong discrimination layer beyond TCR alone, separating patients with similar immune profiles into distinct confidence regions of achieving response
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's computational spine can integrate TCR repertoire dynamics with longitudinal ctDNA kinetics to create a high-resolution predictive model for non-operative management selection.

unresolved_questions: Optimal immunotherapy regimen and duration for MMRd colorectal cancers
Whether sub-clonality, copy number changes, or whole genome doubling correlate with response
rhetorical_signals: proposed that in the super molecular responders, these patients could be considered for non-operative management
propose that this could possibly be used as a tool for real-time clinical decision making in the future

talks.speaker: Dr. Ignacio Melero
talks.institution: University Clinic of Navarra / University of Oxford
talks.title: FAP‑targeted 4‑1BB ligand trimeric costimulation with Cevisatuximab in MSS colorectal cancer

vulnerabilities.mechanistic_blindspot: Limited activity in low‑CEA or low‑FAP tumors; lack of predictive biomarker integration
vulnerabilities.ghost_responder_admission: Speaker highlighted partial responses in a subset (e.g., 5/9 liver metastases) but overall ORR modest

crispro_opportunities.description: Develop a composite biomarker (CEA high + FAP high) to select patients more likely to respond
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted trends for higher CEA and FAP levels in responders
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate tumor expression datasets to define a predictive score for combination benefit
external_validation_needed: TCGA colorectal cancer FAP/CEA expression analysis
ClinicalTrials.gov: retrieve biomarker data from this trial

crispro_opportunities.description: Add checkpoint inhibition (e.g., anti‑PD‑1) to the T‑cell engager + FAP‑4‑1BB regimen to further overcome immune suppression
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker discussed that T‑cell engager alone yields limited responses in immune‑desert tumors
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate triple‑combination dynamics and predict synergistic windows

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal dosing schedule (weekly vs q3‑weeks) for maximal efficacy?
Can FAP expression be used prospectively to enrich for responders?
Will adding anti‑PD‑1 improve response durability?

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the 4‑1BB agonist is conditional on FAP binding, but the platform may be replicated with alternative fibroblast targets
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on fibroblast‑rich tumors; efficacy may be limited in immune‑desert lesions lacking FAP‑positive stroma

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "the combination was considered manageable at the tested doses"
Speaker said: "confirmed overall response rate across cohorts in this study is 13%"

talks.speaker: Dr. Zenara Shah
talks.title: Real-world outcomes of trastuzumab deruxtecan versus standard chemotherapy in HER2‑amplified microsatellite stable metastatic colorectal cancer

vulnerabilities.mechanistic_blindspot: Higher incidence of interstitial lung disease not captured in trial dosing schedules
vulnerabilities.hidden_tox_signal: Speaker noted 12% ILD and 9% heart failure rates, higher than trial reports

crispro_opportunities.description: CrisPRO could help design a prospective registry with standardized HER2 testing and toxicity capture for T‑DXd in mCRC.
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted limitations of EHR coding and lack of adjudicated adverse events.
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO's data‑quality framework can enforce uniform biomarker and safety data capture.

unresolved_questions: Will earlier‑line use of T‑DXd improve outcomes in a prospective trial?

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Efficacy appears limited to later lines; earlier‑line benefit remains hypothesis‑generating
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Use in second‑line without prospective trial may limit regulatory acceptance

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Given the early curve crossing, the hazard ratio should be interpreted with caution."
Speaker said: "We observed slightly higher rates of all grade interstitial lung disease and heart failure than those reported in clinical trials."

talks.speaker: Junyoung Wang
talks.title: NAT10‑MYC loop drives autophagy‑dependent MHC‑I loss and immunotherapy resistance in MSS colorectal cancer

vulnerabilities.mechanistic_blindspot: Autophagy‑mediated degradation of MHC‑I leading to immune evasion

crispro_opportunities.description: Combine NAT10 inhibition with anti‑PD‑1 to restore MHC‑I and overcome resistance in MSS CRC.
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker demonstrated synergistic tumor control with NAT10 knockout plus anti‑PD‑1 in mouse models.
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model autophagy‑MHC‑I axis and predict patient subsets benefiting from NAT10 blockade.

crispro_opportunities.description: Use NAT10 expression or AC4C signature to select MSS CRC patients for combination therapy.
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Higher NAT10 correlated with worse survival and lower immune scores.
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate transcriptomic NAT10 levels with predicted MHC‑I restoration.

unresolved_questions: What is the safety window for systemic NAT10 inhibition?
Can AC4C modulation affect normal tissue protein synthesis?

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker indicated remodeling can inhibit NAT10 but did not disclose specific chemical scaffold, suggesting limited IP differentiation.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on single‑agent NAT10 inhibition without validated predictive biomarker for MSS CRC.

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "NAT10 knockout increased CD8 T‑cell infiltration and enhanced anti‑PD‑1 efficacy up to 80% complete regression."
Speaker said: "Higher NAT10 correlates with lower MHC‑I expression and poorer survival in MSS CRC."

talks.speaker: Dr. Scott Kopetz
talks.institution: University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center
talks.title: RAS and RAF targeting in MSS metastatic colorectal cancer

vulnerabilities.mechanistic_blindspot: Emergence of KRAS, NRAS, MAP2K1 mutations and MET amplifications at progression
vulnerabilities.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted a "fairly high prevalence of KRAS, NRAS, MAP2K1 mutations, as well as some MET amplifications" acquired at progression without detailing why they dominate
vulnerabilities.ghost_responder_admission: Some patients had multiple co‑occurring alterations yet still derived benefit, indicating a subset response

crispro_opportunities.description: Baseline high cell‑cycle transcriptional signature predicts emergence of secondary KRAS/NRAS mutations; CrisPRO could develop a predictive model to select patients who need upfront combination with PI3K or MET inhibitors
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Those that have really high cell cycle signaling on transcriptomic profiling at baseline are the ones that are much more likely to have these secondary mutations"
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: Use CrisPRO's computational spine to integrate baseline RNA‑seq and ctDNA VAF data to generate a risk score for adaptive mutability

crispro_opportunities.description: Pre‑clinical data suggest PI3K‑RAS breaker (e.g., BridgeBio 10203) may overcome resistance mediated by PI3K pathway activation
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The idea, of course, is that normally PI3 kinase, when it's activated through upstream RTKs, will signal down through AKT... we have a covalent inhibitor that prevents the binding of the mutated RAS"
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: Model synergistic effects of KRAS/PI3K breaker combos to prioritize clinical trial designs

crispro_opportunities.description: YAP/TEAD activation identified as a universal resistance pathway after KRAS/RAF inhibition; targeting TEAD could restore sensitivity
crispro_opportunities.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "The universal finding here, nearly universal, was that YAP signaling was indeed up"
crispro_opportunities.crispro_angle: Predict YAP/TEAD activation from transcriptomic signatures and propose TEAD inhibitor combos

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal sequencing of BRAF/EGFR inhibition with chemotherapy to prevent cross‑resistance?
Can YAP/TEAD inhibition synergize with KRAS/RAF inhibitors in patients?
What is the durability of ctDNA clearance as a surrogate for long‑term benefit?

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Encorafenib (BRAF V600E inhibitor) limited to V600E mutation
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that non‑V600E BRAF alterations have limited therapeutic options, exposing a narrow chemical scope
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on combination with EGFR inhibitor and chemotherapy may dilute signal in heterogeneous MSS population

rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we don't have that data formally yet"
Speaker said: "I don't think that MSS will be solved by a single drug, but it will be solved with strategy"
Speaker said: "we still don't have great ideas yet or at least great signals of what to do afterwards"

mmr_deficiency_induction.speaker: Luis Diaz Jr. (MSK)
mmr_deficiency_induction.talk: Chemically Engineering Mismatch Repair Deficiency to Induce Tumor Immunogenicity
mmr_deficiency_induction.key_finding: Temozolomide + cisplatin induces high TMB and MSI score in MSS CRC; patients with high TMB gain and MSI gain survived longer despite trial missing primary endpoint
mmr_deficiency_induction.stc1010_relevance: Validates MSS→MSI conversion as immunotherapy sensitization strategy; STC-1010 hapten approach may achieve similar immunogenicity without chemotherapy toxicity
mmr_deficiency_induction.trial_gap: Missing biomarker: baseline MSH2 promoter methylation to predict MMR conversion; no serial ctDNA monitoring for dynamic MSI tracking
mmr_deficiency_induction.crispro_opportunity: CrisPRO can model which MSS CRC patients are most likely to benefit from immunogenic priming — informing STC-1010 patient selection

top_partners_for_stc1010.rationale: Checkpoint inhibition + whole-cell vaccine: complementary priming (vaccine) vs exhaustion (checkpoint) phases of T-cell response
top_partners_for_stc1010.evidence: Diaz Jr. AACR 2026: dual checkpoint + vaccine synergy in MMR-deficient CRC

top_partners_for_stc1010.rationale: Amplifies CD4 effector response primed by whole-cell vaccine; ICOS co-stimulation synergy
top_partners_for_stc1010.evidence: Allison AACR 2026: CTLA-4 + vaccine ICOS-ligand synergy

top_partners_for_stc1010.rationale: MSS CRC TME costimulation — 4-1BB agonism amplifies T-cell response primed by STC-1010 hapten
top_partners_for_stc1010.evidence: Melero AACR 2026: FAP-4-1BB in MSS CRC, avoids systemic toxicity

top_partners_for_stc1010.rationale: Already in STC-1010 backbone; chemotherapy-induced immunogenic cell death may enhance hapten presentation
Already in STC-1010 backbone; anti-angiogenic normalization of TME may improve T-cell infiltration

mss_crc_specific.name: Cevisatuximab + FAP-4-1BBL trimer (Melero/Inhibrx/Sanofi)
mss_crc_specific.mechanism: FAP-targeted 4-1BB costimulation in MSS CRC TME
mss_crc_specific.notes: Combination partner opportunity rather than direct competitor

mss_crc_specific.name: Temozolomide + cisplatin + PD-1 (Diaz/MSK)
mss_crc_specific.mechanism: Chemical MMR deficiency induction → MSI conversion → PD-1 response
mss_crc_specific.notes: Trial failed primary endpoint; validates immunogenicity concept but not competitive threat

mss_crc_specific.name: Encorafenib + cetuximab (BRAF V600E MSS CRC subset)
mss_crc_specific.mechanism: BRAF/EGFR doublet for BRAF V600E MSS CRC
mss_crc_specific.notes: Different patient population (BRAF V600E ~10% of MSS CRC)

mss_crc_specific.name: KRAS G12C inhibitors (adagrasib, sotorasib)
mss_crc_specific.mechanism: KRAS G12C targeting in MSS CRC
mss_crc_specific.notes: Targeted therapy, not immunotherapy; potential combination partner

identified.question: Optimal immunotherapy regimen and duration for MMRd colorectal cancers
Why do some patients fail to acquire MSI‑high status despite the same drug exposure?
What is the optimal dosing schedule to maximize mutational load while minimizing normal tissue toxicity?

stc1010_specific: No validated biomarker for MSS CRC patients most likely to respond to whole-cell vaccine immunotherapy
MSI conversion rate under chemotherapy backbone (mFOLFOX6) not established as predictive biomarker
Baseline TME immune infiltration (CD8+ T-cell density, CXCL3 immune hubs) not validated as STC-1010 response predictor
ctDNA dynamics as early response biomarker for haptenated vaccine not established
MHC-I expression status (NAT10-MYC axis) as resistance biomarker not integrated into MSS CRC trial designs

top_records.title: Abstract 271: IL-7-secreting Salmonella synergizes with PD-L1 blockade to
top_records.fit_rationale: IL-7-secreting Salmonella synergizes with PD-L1 blockade to induce durable antitumor immune memory, addressing CrisPRO's IMMUNOTHERAPY axis.

top_records.title: Abstract 4191: TP53 dysfunction drives immunometabolic rewiring via mTORC1 activ
top_records.fit_rationale: TP53 dysfunction drives immunometabolic rewiring via mTORC1 activation and autophagy suppression in colorectal cancer

top_records.title: Abstract 3714: Immunopeptidomic discovery of fetal WNT-associated antigen NKD1 e
top_records.fit_rationale: Directly addresses CrisPRO axis with clinical data

top_records.title: Abstract 177: In vivo CRISPR screening uncovers Folr1-mediated metabolic remodel
top_records.fit_rationale: KRAS mutation and Folr1-mediated metabolic remodeling are identified as key factors in CRC resistance to PD-1 blockade

top_records.title: Abstract 454: Overcoming PD-1 resistance with a first-in-class dual-mode agent t
top_records.fit_rationale: The abstract discusses converting pMMR CRC to a dMMR-like state to overcome PD-1 resistance.

top_records.title: Abstract 1816: ACSL4 mediated ferroptosis and its potential role in enhancing th
top_records.fit_rationale: ADC conjugation reprograms payload pharmacodynamics, inducing ferroptosis and immunogenic cell death.

top_records.title: Abstract 6624: Exploiting radiation-induced tumor vulnerabilities via targeted a
top_records.fit_rationale: Targeting radiation-induced HSPA5 translocation from ER to cell surface with anti-HSPA5 ADC.

top_records.title: Abstract 7781: Patient derived tumor-immune organoids capture the functional and
top_records.fit_rationale: Patient-derived tumor-immune organoids predict therapeutic responses in CRC, capturing patient-specific tumor-immune interaction dynamics.

top_records.title: Abstract 663: TOP1i-ADC demonstrates immune-stimulating activity in colorectal c
top_records.fit_rationale: TOP1i-ADC triggers immunogenic changes in cancer cells, fostering immune activation, which is directly related to CrisPRO's IMMUNOTHERAPY axis.

top_records.title: Abstract 7118: TNKS inhibition rewires AMOT-YAP signaling to overcome YAP-driven
top_records.fit_rationale: TNKS inhibition stabilizes AMOT, which sequesters YAP in the cytoplasm, reducing YAP-dependent transcriptional activity and sensitizing KRAS-mutant CRC cells to MEK inhibition.

key_kols.name: Luis A. Diaz Jr.
key_kols.affiliation: Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center, Solid Tumor Oncology Division
key_kols.talk_title: Tumor-agnostic PD-1 blockade, ctDNA‑guided MRD and organ‑preserving immunotherapy
key_kols.relevance_to_stc1010: Chemically engineering MMR deficiency to sensitize MSS CRC to PD-1 blockade — direct mechanistic overlap with whole-cell vaccine immunogenicity strategy

key_kols.name: Dr. Faye Kastrinos
key_kols.affiliation: Columbia University, Department of Medicine, GI Cancer Genetics and Prevention Program
key_kols.talk_title: Genotype‑specific risk assessment and screening strategies for Lynch syndrome
key_kols.relevance_to_stc1010: Lynch syndrome genotype-specific risk — germline MMR deficiency context for MSS vs MSI-H CRC patient selection

key_kols.name: Dr. Ignacio Melero
key_kols.affiliation: University Clinic of Navarra, Spain; University of Oxford, UK
key_kols.talk_title: FAP‑targeted 4‑1BB ligand trimer amplifies T‑cell engager in MSS colorectal cancer
key_kols.relevance_to_stc1010: FAP-targeted 4-1BB in MSS CRC TME — combination partner opportunity for STC-1010 (costimulation + hapten-mediated killing)

key_kols.name: Yanrong Jiang
key_kols.talk_title: Neoadjuvant pembrolizumab stratified by tumor mutation burden and high-risk stage two, three deficient mismatch repair or MSI-high colorectal cancer: The NeoPRISM-CRC study
key_kols.relevance_to_stc1010: NeoPRISM-CRC neoadjuvant pembrolizumab in dMMR/MSI-H — biomarker stratification (TMB, MSI) directly relevant to STC-1010 patient selection

key_kols.name: Junyoung Wang
key_kols.talk_title: NAT10‑MYC loop drives autophagy‑dependent MHC‑I downregulation and immunotherapy resistance in MSS colorectal cancer
